title: tmuxcc client → server-proxy messages
description: All control-plane messages sent by the client to the server-proxy. The client uses the single well-known broker socket to discover sessions, claim session ids, issue lifecycle commands, and — on a data connection — bind to a session with session.attach (D5, tc-4b6k.4).

SessionClaimCommand.description: Claim a named session and return its stable sessionId (D5, tc-4b6k.4). Creates the session if it does not exist and ensures its session-proxy is running. Per-name atomic: concurrent claims are serialized. The response carries { sessionId, name, created } — NO endpoint; the client binds a connection to the session with session.attach. The created flag indicates whether this claim minted the tmux session.

SessionCreateCommand.description: Create a brand-new session with the given name. Fails (session.name-taken) if a session with that name already exists.

SessionCreateUniqueCommand.description: Create a session with a broker-minted unique name derived from baseName (tc-295a.5 / W1.4). The broker uniquifies against its live session table (never a client-side registry) and never silently attaches to an existing session — the response always has created=true and carries the minted name.

workspaceUri.description: Optional workspace identity (S4 / tc-76m8.6). When present the broker sets it on the minted session as the @tmuxcc-workspace user-option so a later reopen matches by identity. Canonical workspace URI; omitted for folderless windows and older extensions.

SessionDestroyCommand.description: Destroy an existing session and reap its session-proxy.

SessionAttachMessage.description: Bind THIS connection to a session's stream (D5, tc-4b6k.4). Sent ONCE after the server-proxy.capabilities handshake on a connection the client wants to become a DATA connection. A top-level client→server-proxy message (NOT a command.request): it transitions the connection rather than returning a correlated response. The broker ensures the session-proxy is running, detaches the connection from the command plane, and hands the transport to the session-proxy — whose snapshot + deltas continue the SAME per-connection seq counter. No second handshake, no per-session socket. Success = the session snapshot arrives; an unknown sessionId yields an error + close. flags carries the reserved D4/D8 tmux-parity client flags (ignore-size/read-only; not acted on in tc-4b6k.4). primaryPaneId is the targeted-attach hint (formerly the pane.attach command, tc-7xv.36).

ServerProxyInfoCommand.description: Read-only server-proxy diagnostics snapshot (tc-k6v). No side-effects. Older server-proxies respond with protocol.unknown-message.

ServerProxyCommand.description: Discriminated union of all server-proxy commands a client may issue via command.request. Narrow on `kind`. (session.attach is a top-level message, not a command — see SessionAttachMessage.)

ServerProxyCommandRequestMessage.description: Client issues a server-proxy-level command. direction: client→server-proxy.

correlationId.description: Client-generated opaque string, echoed in the matching response.

ServerProxyClientMessage.description: Discriminated union of all messages the client sends to the server-proxy. Note: ClientCapabilitiesMessage is shared between both wires. session.attach (D5) transitions a data connection into the session-proxy stream.
